evals.prompt:
Generate a briefing from these three survived findings for our weekly stakeholder sync and write it to briefing.md. Topic is 'LLM inference cost trends', period is 2026-06-12 to 2026-06-19.

Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_cost_1 | title: H100 spot prices fell 18% MoM | dimension: cost | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/gpu-prices-june2026
- id: urn:mif:f_cost_2 | title: Speculative decoding cuts token latency 2x on Llama-3 class models | dimension: technical | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/spec-decode-bench
- id: urn:mif:f_cost_3 | title: AWS Bedrock introduced per-token caching in us-east-1 | dimension: landscape | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/aws-bedrock-cache
evals.expected_output: A terse one-page briefing written to briefing.md with Headline, What's New (3 bullets with inline citations), Why It Matters (one line each), What's Next / Asks section, and front matter (title, date, period). No falsified findings. Citations use [1]/[2]/[3] markers or bare links — NOT raw urn:mif: ids in the body text.

deterministic_checks.description: Output contains a What's New section
Output contains a Why It Matters section
Output contains a What's Next / Asks section
Front matter references the coverage period dates
Citations appear as inline markers or bare links — not raw urn:mif: in prose
Internal MIF URNs do not appear in the body text

expectations.description: The briefing body does not expose raw urn:mif:f_cost_1 / urn:mif:f_cost_2 / urn:mif:f_cost_3 identifiers directly in the prose text (they must be resolved to [N] markers or footnotes)
expectations.criteria: The text rendered to the user must NOT contain 'urn:mif:f_' as visible prose. Citation IDs must be translated to [1], [2], [3] or equivalent footnote form.

expectations.description: All three survived findings appear in What's New as distinct bullets
expectations.criteria: Three separate bullet points covering H100 spot prices, speculative decoding, and AWS Bedrock per-token caching, each traceable to the input findings.

expectations.description: The output is a terse one-pager — it does not pad with background context about LLM inference, does not re-explain the topic from scratch, and focuses on the delta
expectations.criteria: The briefing is concise and delta-focused. There is no lengthy introduction re-explaining what LLM inference is. Background is assumed. Approximately 200-400 words total.

evals.prompt:
Create a briefing for the Monday standup and write it to briefing.md. Topic: 'open-source AI governance'. Period: 2026-06-16 to 2026-06-19.

Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_gov_1 | title: EU AI Act enforcement unit published its first draft compliance template | dimension: regulatory | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/eu-ai-act-template
- id: urn:mif:f_gov_2 | title: Mistral AI released Mixtral-8x22B under Apache 2.0 contrary to earlier reports | dimension: landscape | verdict: falsified | citation: https://example.com/mistral-correction
- id: urn:mif:f_gov_3 | title: White House issued EO draft mandating model cards for federal procurement | dimension: regulatory | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/whitehouse-eo-draft
evals.expected_output: Briefing written to briefing.md includes the two survived findings (f_gov_1 and f_gov_3) but completely excludes the falsified finding (f_gov_2 / Mixtral Apache 2.0 claim). The falsified finding must not appear in What's New.

deterministic_checks.description: Survived finding f_gov_1 (EU AI Act template) is included
Survived finding f_gov_3 (White House EO draft) is included
Required What's Next / Asks section present
Internal MIF URNs do not appear in the output

expectations.description: The briefing must NOT contain the falsified finding about Mixtral-8x22B being under Apache 2.0
expectations.criteria: The claim that Mistral AI released Mixtral-8x22B under Apache 2.0 must not appear anywhere in What's New or Why It Matters. Falsified findings are excluded entirely — they should not be mentioned even as 'this turned out to be wrong'.

expectations.description: Why It Matters has one line per item, paired to each What's New bullet — no orphan bullet without a paired implication
expectations.criteria: Each item in What's New is matched by a corresponding implication in Why It Matters. With 2 survived findings, there should be exactly 2 Why It Matters entries.

evals.prompt:
Please write a briefing for the exec sync to briefing.md. Topic: 'vector database market'. Period: 2026-05-01 to 2026-06-19.

Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_vdb_1 | title: Pinecone raised Series C at $750M valuation | dimension: landscape | verdict: weakened | citation: https://example.com/pinecone-series-c | note: disconfirming evidence found that the valuation figure may be $650M
- id: urn:mif:f_vdb_2 | title: Weaviate added native sparse-dense hybrid search | dimension: technical | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/weaviate-hybrid
- id: urn:mif:f_vdb_3 | title: pgvector 0.8 released with HNSW index compression | dimension: technical | verdict: inconclusive | citation: https://example.com/pgvector-08
evals.expected_output: Briefing written to briefing.md includes all three findings (weakened and inconclusive ones are flagged inline, not excluded), with f_vdb_1 flagged as uncertain and f_vdb_3 flagged as inconclusive. Survived finding f_vdb_2 presented without qualification.

deterministic_checks.description: Survived finding f_vdb_2 (Weaviate hybrid search) is present without qualification
Weakened finding f_vdb_1 (Pinecone valuation) is present (weakened means included with flag, not excluded)
Internal MIF URNs do not appear in the output

expectations.description: The weakened Pinecone valuation finding is flagged inline as uncertain or unconfirmed — not presented as settled fact
expectations.criteria: The Pinecone valuation bullet in What's New or its Why It Matters entry includes a qualifier such as 'unconfirmed', 'reported', 'contested', 'may be', or similar — signaling the weakened verdict to the reader without burying the finding.

expectations.description: The inconclusive pgvector finding is flagged inline as unverified / inconclusive, not presented as settled fact
expectations.criteria: The pgvector 0.8 bullet carries an inline qualification (e.g., 'unverified', 'inconclusive', 'pending confirmation') so the exec audience knows the confidence level.

expectations.description: The survived Weaviate finding is presented without any uncertainty qualifier — it passed falsification so no hedging is warranted
expectations.criteria: The Weaviate hybrid search bullet contains no hedging language ('reportedly', 'may have', 'unconfirmed'). It is stated as fact.

evals.prompt:
I need a briefing from these research findings. Write it to briefing.md. Topic: 'GPU supply chain'. Period: 2026-06-01 to 2026-06-19.

Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_gpu_1 | title: TSMC 3nm capacity fully booked through Q1 2027 | dimension: supply | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/tsmc-3nm-capacity
- id: urn:mif:f_gpu_2 | title: NVIDIA H200 shipments delayed 6 weeks due to CoWoS packaging constraint | dimension: supply | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/h200-delay
- id: urn:mif:f_gpu_3 | title: AMD MI300X production ramping ahead of schedule | dimension: supply | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/mi300x-ramp
- id: urn:mif:f_gpu_4 | title: Samsung HBM3e rejected by NVIDIA quality gate for second consecutive quarter | dimension: supply | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/samsung-hbm3e
evals.expected_output: A terse one-page briefing written to briefing.md covering the 4 survived findings. Output must be tight and decision-focused — not a lengthy essay. The What's Next / Asks section must name at least one concrete next action or decision needed, not a generic 'continue monitoring'.

deterministic_checks.description: Internal MIF URNs do not appear in the output

expectations.description: The What's Next / Asks section names a concrete action or decision — not just 'continue monitoring' or 'stay tuned'
expectations.criteria: The What's Next section contains at least one specific action item: e.g., a procurement decision, a vendor evaluation, a date to revisit, or a named stakeholder ask. Generic watch/monitor statements without specificity do not satisfy this.

expectations.description: The briefing is concise enough to fit one page — does not pad with supply-chain background knowledge that the recurring exec audience already holds
expectations.criteria: The briefing is approximately 300-450 words or fewer. It does not explain what CoWoS packaging is, what HBM is, or other background that a recurring GPU supply-chain audience is assumed to know.
